prerequisite_rows.note: checkpoint, spill-tape, and installed-process lifecycle artifacts exist, so resumable continuation objects are real under explicit envelopes
effect receipts and typed refusal boundaries are explicit on the current bounded effect and hybrid surfaces
hybrid control is only counted when verifier-positive-delta cases and unsupported-transition refusals are both explicit
effective-unbounded claims require more than the article-closeout portability anchor; today the broader portability matrix is still too narrow
the broad internal-compute publication gate must be green before any effective-unbounded claim can be disclosed safely
effective-unbounded claims require specialization families to stay decompilable and safe to challenge, not just benchmarked

meaning_statement: effective unbounded computation here means bounded execution slices plus resumable state, continuation, and refusal-safe effect handling under declared portability envelopes and explicit checkpoint or tape objects; it does not mean infinite in-core execution or arbitrary universality
claim_boundary: this checker is a disclosure-safe claim-closure surface. It decides whether the repo may honestly say `effective unbounded computation under envelopes`, and it keeps that claim suppressed until portability, specialization safety, and broad publication prerequisites are all green
summary: Effective-unbounded claim checker records satisfied_prerequisites=3, missing_prerequisites=3, claim_status=Suppressed.
